buy_side_bullets: AI bull market has 1-2 more years to run — 1999 parallel holds, adding to positions. [Tudor Jones]
Quality stocks 'stupidly cheap,' 10x potential; 10-year equity index still the best bet. [Ackman]
Fed cannot cut — may hike; secular rate decline over; DoubleLine at 17-year lowest risk. [Gundlach]
AI is not a fad but current prices demand rigorous valuation discipline — don't confuse theme with price. [Marks]
Buying beaten-down value (Flutter, DraftKings, JD.com) while shorting AI leaders (NVIDIA, Palantir puts). [Burry]
sell_side_bullets: GDP near 3% and earnings up 25% YTD, but consumers strained by negative real wages — fault lines beneath the surface. [Sonders]
Financial repression for 15 years: 4-5.5% inflation, suppressed rates; post-1979 investment playbook is obsolete. [Napier]
whats_new: Sonders July Market Snapshot upgrades full-year earnings growth forecast to 25% — first explicit upward revision this cycle
Ackman's index fund endorsement is a new stance — first time the concentrated stock-picker publicly advocates passive as top 10-year bet
Gundlach shifts from 'no cuts' to 'may hike' — more hawkish than consensus and prior positioning
Burry's new longs (Flutter, DraftKings, JD.com) represent fresh sector calls — gaming and China value not in prior disclosures
Napier makes financial repression the dominant framework for 15+ years — most extended horizon call in this cycle
vs_market_buy: Gundlach: market prices 0 rate hikes; he says a hike is more likely than any cut — extreme divergence from consensus
Burry: market is consensus-long AI; he is short NVIDIA/Palantir while buying gaming/China — direct contra-momentum
vs_market_sell: Napier: market expects eventual return to low inflation/normal rates; he says repression suppresses rates for 15 years — no return to normal
buy_vs_sell_divergence: Rates trajectory: Gundlach (buy-side) says hike coming; Hartnett (sell-side) says 5% yields already signal sell
Equity outlook: Ackman, Tudor Jones bullish on quality/AI; Bannister calls for 5,250-6,500 correction by Q3
AI sustainability: Tudor Jones and Marks see productive multi-year runway; Burry shorts NVIDIA/Palantir as overvalued
Inflation: Edwards and Napier see persistent/rising; Sonders and Hatzius see sticky but manageable
aligned: AI is a structural, multi-year macro force — not a fad [Marks, Slok, Hatzius, Tudor Jones]
Economy has held up better than feared — GDP resilient, earnings strong [Sonders, Bessent, Tepper, Hatzius]
Fiscal deficit trajectory is unsustainable — long-term risk to dollar and bonds [Bessent, Napier, Dalio, Mauldin]
Concentration risk in US equities is elevated — Mag 7 dominance is a vulnerability [Hartnett, Wilson, Berezin]
buy_side_trades: Long AI-related equities — Jones added to positions [Paul Tudor Jones]
Watch valuation levels: 300%+ market cap/GDP ratio is a key risk-off trigger [Paul Tudor Jones]
Selective within private credit — avoid sectors where capital deployment outpaced underwriting discipline [Howard Marks]
AI exposure requires price discipline — assess valuations, not just growth narrative [Howard Marks]
30% non-US stocks, 20% real assets, 30% high-quality bonds, 20% cash — Gundlach's recommended allocation [Jeffrey Gundlach]
Short long-duration Treasuries / avoid duration risk — yields will keep rising [Jeffrey Gundlach]
sell_side_trades: Avoid long-duration nominal bonds — returns will be structurally negative in real terms [Russell Napier]
Real assets, equities, gold as inflation protection [Russell Napier]
Equity markets as financial repression beneficiary — suppressed rates inflate asset values even with high inflation [Russell Napier]
Selective equity exposure with attention to concentration risk — not just buy the index blindly [Liz Ann Sonders]
Watch consumer health indicators — if real wages stay negative, spending slowdown likely [Liz Ann Sonders]
dominant_themes: AI Productivity Boom vs. Valuation Risk — multi-year tailwind with potential bubble formation
Rates Higher for Longer / Possible Hike — consensus cuts priced out, Gundlach and Napier flagging structural shift
Fiscal Debt & Financial Repression — unsustainable deficits driving 15-year repression regime (Napier, Dalio, Mauldin)

Long Brazil assets (R$33B institutional inflow theme) [Stan Druckenmiller]
Diversify away from US dollar assets — non-US equities and bonds [Ray Dalio]
Long gold and real assets as Big Cycle hedge [Ray Dalio]

sell_side_trades: Reduce equity concentration; shift toward 25/25/25/25 stocks/bonds/cash/commodities [Michael Hartnett]
Monitor Japan bank stocks as early warning for global correction [Michael Hartnett]
Avoid long-duration bonds — fiscal dominance creates persistent inflation risk premium [Albert Edwards]
Long inflation protection: TIPS, commodities, real assets [Albert Edwards]
Long bonds on disinflation-driven Fed rate-cut narrative [David Rosenberg]
Rosie Macro ETF launching as publicly listed vehicle [David Rosenberg]
